target.name: Physical Intelligence
target.founder: Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, Brian Ichter

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Physical Intelligence, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, and Brian Ichter, is valued at $5.6B post-money following $600M+ in total fundraising. The valuation triangulation draws from a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR), with primary analysis anchored to 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers: Figure AI ($39.0B Series C), Skild AI ($14.0B Series C), and Nominal ($1.0B Series B).

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $10.6B from the current $5.6B post-money base. Peer-median analysis across the 3-company set produces a median valuation of $14.0B, with a range of $1.0B to $39.0B reflecting substantial dispersion in the peer cohort. The peer-median approach reflects the higher end of observable fundraising outcomes at this stage and sector.

The two methods triangulate to a valuation range of $9.0B (low) to $16.1B (high), with a median of $12.3B. This range sits above the current valuation, consistent with stage progression expectations. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the $39.0B Figure AI comp, substantial valuation dispersion within the peer set, and limited public market data for direct calibration.
